Article Title: What happened to Emily Awbrey after her Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders dream finally came true
Article Text:
Emily Awbrey in 'AMERICA'S SWEETHEARTS: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders' season 3. (Instagram and Netflix)

Who is Emily Awbrey and where is she from?

Why was Emily Awbrey cut from DCC training camp?

What is Emily Awbrey doing now in 2026?

Season 3 of "America's Sweethearts: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders" dropped on Netflix on June 16, and one storyline already has fans talking: Oregon dancer Emily Awbrey's journey through the brutal audition gauntlet. She made it through 81 finalists down to 44 training camp hopefuls, only to be cut before claiming a spot on the 2025-26 squad. Now, after a year of grinding, she's back.Awbrey grew up in Medford, Oregon, dancing almost as soon as she could walk. Her mom runs Fusion Dance Academy in Grants Pass, so the studio basically raised her. She trained in ballet, contemporary and modern styles, and started choreographing and teaching dance while she was still young herself. "It really became a second home for me," she said of the studio in a September 2025 Facebook post.From there, she headed to the University of Arizona and earned her Bachelor of Fine Arts in May 2024. Rather than settling into a typical post-grad routine, she signed on with Royal Caribbean and spent nearly a year dancing professionally on cruise ships, right up until her DCC audition pulled her in a different direction."The cruise ship life is like no other," she said on the show. "It's been the craziest experience ever. "Coming out of finals, Awbrey looked like a lock. Director Kelli Finglass called her "a brilliant dancer" and went so far as to say she was "one of the strongest dance technicians in the entire audition." But training camp is a different animal, and Awbrey struggled almost immediately to lock in the DCC's signature pom style. She became the first dancer Finglass and Trammell pulled aside for a hard conversation. "We're not seeing the confidence that we saw at finals," Finglass told her. "But we see the potential, otherwise we wouldn't be having this conversation."She survived two rounds of cuts. Cowboys co-owner Charlotte Jones even stepped in to help evaluate the remaining dancers and was blunt about Awbrey's high kicks, calling them "the best kicks on the field by far." It wasn't enough. Awbrey was let go in the third round, one of three cut that day. Finglass told her she'd been a "personal favorite," and both coaches pushed her to come back. "I'd be disappointed if you don't try out again," Finglass said. Trammell added, "You've got us on your side." Awbrey didn't hesitate. "I would love to re-audition," she told them. "And I absolutely will see you back at round one."After the cut, Awbrey leaned on her support system, traveling to Nashville with her boyfriend Jackson and making a trip back to Dallas to finally watch the Cowboys play. "Finally got to watch my girls in action," she posted. She also returned home to teach hip hop, lyrical, contemporary and jazz at Fusion Dance Academy. "I couldn't be happier to be back teaching and choreographing for our students this year," she wrote.But she never stopped working toward a second shot. Awbrey took DCC prep classes in February, submitted her online audition in March, and spent months drilling the pom technique that tripped her up the first time. It paid off. This June, she made it back to finals and was named one of 14 rookie candidates heading into 2026-27 training camp, putting her right back in the running for a spot on the squad.
Article Summary: Emily Awbrey appeared on "America's Sweethearts: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders" She made it through 81 finalists down to 44 training camp hopefuls. But she was cut before claiming a spot on the 2025-26 squad.

Article Title: Will Taylor Swift perform at her own wedding? Here's what insiders are saying
Article Text:
Taylor Swift wedding (Getty Images)

Is a Taylor Swift performance in her own wedding going to happen?

Speculation is building that Taylor Swift won't just be the bride at her own wedding, she might also take the mic. Multiple entertainment outlets reported this week that the singer is quietly preparing a live performance for her celebration with Travis Kelce, expected to take place at Madison Square Garden on July 3. The buzz follows a late-night studio session in New York and new details about a massive stage being built in Pennsylvania.The rumor mill kicked into gear after Rob Shuter's Naughty But Nice Substack reported on June 17 that Taylor Swift, 36, is planning something musical for her big day. "Taylor isn't planning a traditional wedding," one source told the outlet. "She's creating a once-in-a-lifetime experience. People close to her fully expect some kind of musical moment."That theory picked up steam after E! News reported Swift was spotted at Electric Lady Studios in Manhattan this past Monday night, arriving around 8:30 p.m. and not leaving until 6 a.m. the next morning. She wasn't alone, either. Alana and Danielle Haim, her longtime friends and frequent collaborators, were reportedly with her the whole time.A second source pushed back on the idea that the session was routine. "Nobody believes Taylor was sitting in a recording studio for no reason," they said."The expectation among her inner circle is that she's preparing something special for the wedding weekend." A third insider added that surprising guests would fit Swift's personality perfectly: "Taylor loves surprises, and she loves creating unforgettable moments. Performing for the people she loves on one of the biggest nights of her life would be completely on-brand for her."Meanwhile, TMZ reported that crews are building a "massive stage" under heavy security at Rock Lititz, a production campus in Pennsylvania known for outfitting major concert tours. The timing only adds fuel to the fire, especially with the wedding rumored for Friday, July 3, at Madison Square Garden, according to In Touch.New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani didn't confirm details, but he didn't dodge the topic either. Asked about a packed holiday weekend that includes a FIFA World Cup match, the Knicks' playoff run, and America's 250th birthday, he name-checked the wedding directly. "We know it coincides with the Knicks' finals run. We know it coincides with July 4, America 250, Taylor Swift's wedding, all happening at the same time, and we are so excited to welcome the world here," he said.Nothing has been confirmed by Taylor Swift or Kelce themselves. But between the studio sessions, the secretive stage build, and a mayor casually confirming the date in public, it's getting harder to write this off as just another rumor.
Article Summary: Speculation is building that Taylor Swift will perform at her wedding. The singer is reportedly preparing a live performance for her celebration. The wedding is expected to take place at Madison Square Garden on July 3. The buzz follows a late-night studio session in New York and new details about a massive stage.

Article Title: Why was Dayton Bramhall cut from the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders? Inside her emotional fourth rejection and where she is now
Article Text:
Dayton Bramhall's dream of becoming a Dallas Cowboys Cheerleader ended after she was cut for a fourth time during the 2025-26 auditions, despite months of intensive training. Judges praised her dedication but felt her performance style did not fit the team. Dayton has since embraced a new chapter, continuing to dance while pursuing Broadway aspirations and reflecting on her journey as one of growth, resilience, and self-discovery.

Where is Dayton Bramhall now after her Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders journey?

Dayton Bramhall's fourth Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders audition ends before training camp

One of the most emotional stories to come out of America’s Sweethearts: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders has been Dayton Bramhall’s story of going after a Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders uniform. Years of auditions, setbacks and relentless training have brought her to her latest chapter, leaving fans wondering what’s next.The daughter of longtime Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders associate choreographer Shelly Bramhall returned for another shot during the 2025-26 auditions. Instead of the fairytale ending many expected, Dayton's journey took another painful turn. Yet her story did not end when the auditions did.Although she is no longer pursuing a place on the Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders, Dayton has not stepped away from dance. She continues to train regularly, shares videos from dance studios on social media and has previously taught dance classes, showing that performing remains central to her life.ID@undefinedCaption not available.Following her final audition, Dayton reflected on the experience in a personal essay, confirming that her fourth attempt would also be her last."I am closing that chapter and opening up a new one," she wrote. "I am so glad that I gave it another shot because I’ve learned so many things about myself. I’ve grown so much these past two years, just from this experience alone. That was enough for me, and now it’s time to move on to the next thing and grow somewhere else."She also revealed that dance still shapes her future ambitions."The world is my oyster," Dayton wrote. "The biggest thing I would love to do is dance on Broadway and in the West End in London."Outside her career, she appears to have embraced a fresh start. Her social media posts highlight personal growth, international travel, new friendships and a relationship with Drew Goss. Although she still lives in Houston, she has spent considerable time visiting London, a city that aligns with her professional dreams.Season 3 documented Dayton's determination after being cut during training camp the previous year. She explained that she had spent months improving every part of her performance."I've been in class every single week, maybe three times a week, working on style, working on confidence and performance," she said. "I feel more confident this year. I feel more myself, more grown up, so I don't feel like I'm trying to cut and paste into a different mold."She added, "I'm just being me, and that's making it a whole lot easier to get through this round. I'm so happy to be here and happy to get another chance."Despite her preparation, Dayton was eliminated during the final round before training camp.ID@undefinedCaption not available.Director Kelli Finglass admitted the decision was difficult, saying, "I can sleep at night cause I know that we gave her every opportunity, and she did herself too. She's worked hard. It's just sad that it didn't work out. Dayton is a beautiful girl, and she's a beautiful dancer. The power and the ability to captivate and excite and ignite the space in which she's dancing, [...] I don't think that's her style of dance."Head choreographer Judy Trammell called it "one of the hardest things in my career," explaining that avoiding another training camp cut was ultimately the kinder decision.Dayton later responded to a supportive fan on TikTok with remarkable honesty."Thank you for being so sweet, Charlotte! It wasn't in the cards for me, but I'm proud of how much i've grown. I feel it's important to note I did EVERYTHING they asked of me and tried my absolute best to fit their mold. I'm not wanted there and I'm hoping this takes me to where I truly belong!"While her Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders dream has ended, Dayton Bramhall's resilience has earned admiration from viewers who believe her next opportunity may be even bigger than the one she chased for years.
Article Summary: Dayton Bramhall's dream of becoming a Dallas Cowboys Cheerleader ended after she was cut for a fourth time during the 2025-26 auditions. Judges praised her dedication but felt her performance style did not fit the team. Dayton has since embraced a new chapter, continuing to dance while pursuing Broadway aspirations.

Article Title: Who is Jenna Waller? Fan favourite Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders rookie finally earns her place after heartbreaking cut
Article Text:
Jenna Waller has officially earned a place on the 2025 Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders after narrowly missing the team in 2024, as seen on Netflix's America's Sweethearts. A University of Oklahoma graduate and lifelong dancer, Waller's perseverance has inspired fans. Beyond dance, she supports Operation Smile, inspired by her own cleft lip journey. Her advice remains: "Work hard, stay true to yourself, and trust the process. Control the controllable and let fate handle the rest!"

Who is Jenna Waller? Meet the new Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders rookie

Jenna Waller officially joins the 2025 Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders after emotional setback

Jenna Waller’s story has come full circle. After coming up just one spot shy of becoming a Dallas Cowboys Cheerleader in 2024, the fan favorite has officially made the 2025 squad. Her emotional return has become one of the biggest talking points among viewers of America’s Sweethearts: Netflix: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders.The announcement arrived through social media, where fellow cheerleader Charly Barby shared a celebratory TikTok featuring the newest members of the team. For fans who watched Waller's painful exit last season, the update felt like the ending they had been hoping for.Born and raised in San Diego, California, Jenna Waller has spent nearly her entire life preparing for a career in dance. She began dancing at just two years old before moving into competitive dance at age three. She later spent four years on her high school's dance team, sharpening the skills that would eventually take her to one of the country's most recognizable cheerleading programs.ID@undefinedCaption not available.Her passion continued at the University of Oklahoma, where she joined the school's renowned Pom Squad. The team performs jazz and hip-hop routines at football, basketball, wrestling and other university events, giving Waller years of experience performing in front of large crowds.She graduated in May 2024 with a bachelor's degree in sociology.Her official Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders profile also offers a glimpse into her personality. Waller lists Young Zac Efron and Barack Obama as her celebrity crushes, while Midnight in Paris tops her favorite movie list. She enjoys comfort shows including Grey's Anatomy, New Girl and The Vampire Diaries.Away from dance, Waller is a devoted pet lover. She owns a cat named Norman and also adores her family's pets Joey, Gus, Benny and Zeke. If she could invite anyone to dinner, she says it would be Drew Barrymore because she "thinks we'd have a blast."She also shared some fun facts about herself, revealing she can recite the alphabet backward and is double-jointed. More importantly, Waller opened up about being born with a cleft lip and having one artificial front tooth. That personal experience inspired her to support Operation Smile, a charity that provides care for children born with cleft conditions.ID@undefinedCaption not available.Netflix viewers watched Waller endure one of Season 2's toughest moments when she became the final candidate cut from the 2024 Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders roster. Director Kelli Finglass delivered the difficult news while also encouraging her future, telling Jenna she believed she "peaked at the right time for a strong impression for the next time."Those words proved prophetic.Less than a year later, Waller finished her comeback, earning an official spot on the 2025 Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders squad. The news spread quickly on social media, with fans celebrating a story of resilience and not disappointment.Her story rings true because it mirrors the reality of elite auditions. "Sometimes talent is not enough. And it is persistence that often makes the difference.Waller has brought that philosophy into the next chapter of her career. When asked what advice she would give to hopeful Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders, her answer is a perfect reflection of her own journey:"Work hard, stay true to yourself, and trust the process. Control the controllable and let fate handle the rest!"To many fans, Jenna Waller is no longer remembered as the rookie who just missed the team. Now she’s the cheerleader who refused to let one setback define her, turning heartbreak into one of the most inspiring comeback stories of the 2025 Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders season.
Article Summary: Jenna Waller has officially earned a place on the 2025 Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders after narrowly missing the team in 2024. A University of Oklahoma graduate and lifelong dancer, Waller's perseverance has inspired fans. Her advice remains: "Work hard, stay true to yourself, and trust the process"

Article Title: "The wedding is just a marketing tool": Fans erupt over Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's rumored Madison Square Garden wedding event

Supporters defended the couple, reminding critics they are not invited. With no official confirmation, the reported plans continue fueling widespread speculation and viral reactions.

Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding rumors divide fans online

Madison Square Garden wedding report triggers criticism and skepticism

New reports are flying on social media that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are planning a private wedding ceremony, followed by a star-studded celebration at Madison Square Garden. Neither Swift nor Kelce have confirmed the reports but the speculation alone has set off a wave of reactions from excitement to outright disbelief.The alleged plans, which suggest a private ceremony before a larger July 3 celebration featuring musical performances, quickly became one of the internet's biggest talking points. As a wedding gift to Travis?"Others simply embraced the excitement, with one person posting, "This wedding of celebs is always fantastic."However, many social media users questioned the scale of the reported event.One viral reaction said, "Everything about this woman is so fabricated. Her wedding is also just a media circus."Another user joked, "she’s having a sangeet? oh taylor singh.."A different commenter wrote, "The levels of parasocialism this will cause if it’s true 😭😭😭."As the rumor continued spreading, critics argued that a venue like Madison Square Garden felt excessive for a wedding celebration. Not everyone entertained the backlash. One fan responded to the growing criticism with a blunt reminder: "Why are all acting so weird ,you are not invited."For now, the reported wedding celebration remains unconfirmed. Article Title: Blake Lively's life takes a turn for the worse weeks ahead of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
Article Text:
Blake Lively's life takes a turn for the worse weeks ahead of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding.(Image via Getty Images)

Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds face a new issue in their lives days ahead of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding



Are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce inviting Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds to their big fat wedding?



Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have had a lot to deal with in their lives concerning the billionaire pop star’s strained bond with her former best friend, Blake Lively As rumors swirl about Blake Lively being cut off from Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s lavish wedding, it seems the actress is facing a new issue in her life.A few days ago, as per Rob Shuter, it seems Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds’ reputation has gone for a toss after they missed out on paying the contractors working on their house on time.As per Rob Shuter, the source said, “People aren’t lining up for this job anymore…The money may be there, but the trust isn’t.”The source added, “They can absolutely afford to finish the house…The issue isn’t wealth. The issue is confidence.”As per Rob Shuter, the source continued, “The bills may be settled, but people remember how they got settled…Contractors had to file liens to get paid. That sends a message, and it’s not one the industry forgets.”While nothing has been confirmed yet, it seems things might just be getting worse for the Hollywood power couple, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds.For now, all eyes remain on whether Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce will be inviting Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds to their high profile wedding after being distant from them for over a year.As per Page Six, sources close to Taylor Swift have revealed that she is in no mood to work on her friendship with Blake Lively.However, many fans feel that the billionaire pop star might have a change of heart at the end and end up inviting Blake Lively to her wedding.For now, it is being rumored that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce will tie the knot on 3rd July at Madison Square Garden in New York City.Though the location has come as a huge shock to many since fans believed that Taylor Swift would have wanted a more private wedding, sources reveal that a huge stage is already being set up at the venue.Neither Taylor Swift nor Travis Kelce has responded to the speculations yet.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have had a lot to deal with in their lives concerning the billionaire pop star’s strained bond with her former best friend, Blake Lively. It seems Lively and Ryan Reynolds’ reputation has gone for a toss after they missed out on paying the contractors working on their house on time.

Article Title: "They don't understand the mom": Hailee Steinfeld breaks silence on the dark side of postpartum as Josh Allen focuses on recovery
Article Text:
Hailee Steinfeld breaks silence on the dark side of postpartum as Josh Allen focuses on recovery.(Image via Getty Images)

Hailee Steinfeld shares how postpartum changed her as Josh Allen cheers her on



Josh Allen opens up about how the Buffalo Bills are gearing up for the next season as Hailee Steinfeld takes a break



Josh Allen and Hailee Steinfeld have always been pretty private about their lives and ever since the two welcomed their daughter into the world, it seems they have amped up their security.While both the Buffalo Bills’ popular quarterback Josh Allen and BAFTA nominated actress Hailee Steinfeld are yet to announce their daughter’s name in public, she has opened up about the dark side of postpartum.A few weeks ago, for a recent issue of her popular newsletter Beau Society, the “Sinners” star opened up about reading The First 40 Days as she wrote, "Every word has resonated…”The BAFTA nominated actress added, "There's a paragraph early on that talks about how even forty days after giving birth, you're still in such a vulnerable state. People understand that about your new baby, but they don't understand that about you, the mom."The “True Grit” actress continued, "I'm still in this early period…and need to remember to be super gentle with myself in every way — physically, mentally, nutritionally, spiritually, all of it."Hailee Steinfeld and Josh Allen have yet to post pictures of their daughter’s face and reveal her name in the media as it seems both are strictly private about it.As for the Buffalo Bills’ star Josh Allen, many are expecting him to bounce back on the field in the next few months after he sustained a brutal metatarsal avulsion fracture in his right foot in the last season.Josh Allen seems to be focusing on fully recovering ahead of the new season as he recently spoke about the team’s vision last week at the Buffalo Bills’ minicamp.The Buffalo Bills’ popular quarterback said, "Connect, compete, condition. And then come out here and just have a lot of fun and play some football."Josh Allen added, "We're competing at a very high level…Guys are flying around; we've done a good job of connecting."For now, it seems Hailee Steinfeld is taking a break in her career as she prefers to spend time with their newborn while Josh Allen gears up for the next season.
Article Summary: Hailee Steinfeld has opened up about the dark side of postpartum. The BAFTA nominated actress and Josh Allen have yet to announce their daughter’s name in public. Josh Allen seems to be focusing on fully recovering ahead of the new season as he recently spoke about the team's vision last week.

He wore a matte-black suit, while she wore a velvet-black gown.Jalen and Bry got engaged in Sept. 2024.“I knew a long time ago, I mean to this point in my life, that’s an irreplaceable feeling.I think that’s what allowed us to get where we are now,” Hurts told ESSENCE in May 2023.ESSENCE reported that the star quarterback popped the heart-fluttering question in a romantic setting on a beach during a vacation before the NFL season began.He gave her support a shoutout at a press conference before the 2025 Super Bowl.“To Bry, the Mrs., thank you for the support, thank you for the love, and thank you for being the rock that I can lean on,” Hurts said.When the Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs in the Super Bowl in Feb. 2025, Burrows and Hurts shared a celebratory kiss on the gridiron.Following the Super Bowl LIX win, they got married in the spring of 2025.“You can call her my wife,” Hurts told Men’s Health for the magazine’s May/June 2025 cover story.The NFL power couple got married for the second time at the Four Seasons hotel in Napa Valley, California, in June 2025.They reserved the entire hotel from June 12 to June 15, 2025. Article Title: From double heartbreak to happily ever after: How two failed romances with Joe Alwyn and Matty Healy led Taylor Swift straight to Travis Kelce
Article Text:
From double heartbreak to happily ever after: How two failed relationships with Joe Alwyn and Matty Healy led Taylor Swift straight to Travis Kelce (Image via X: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce)

Eras Tour saved Taylor Swift from Joe Alwyn and Matty Healy and led her to savior Travis Kelce

From The Tortured Poets Department to The Life of a Showgirl

Matty Healy was probably Taylor Swift ’s rebound from a six-year relationship with Joe Alwyn The rebound is never permanent.It’s the relationship that comes after a rebound that becomes serious.Then you’re over your ex and you’re also over your rebound.It all happened too fast in 2023. Taylor Swift broke up with Joe Alwyn.She got into a relationship with Matty Healy. And then she broke up with Healy.Then came her 2023 Eras Tour. Where her ‘happily ever after’ aka Travis Kelce came into the picture.The billionaire superstar even addressed her two painful breakups in her Disney+ documentary – the End of an Era.“There were points in this tour when the tour was really the only thing that was really keeping me going in my life,” Swift said.“But there were never points in the tour where I thought, ‘Oh, you know what? I want to quit the tour because the tour’s hard.’No, my personal life was hard. I went through two breakups on the first half of this tour, and that’s a lot of breakups actually.The show was what gave me purpose and was what I could use to get me out of bed. So, the tour has never really been the hard thing in my life.The tour has been the thing that has allowed me to find purpose outside of the sh*t that was going wrong in my life.Men will let you down, the Eras Tour will never.”And in that Eras Tour when she was looking for her purpose in life, someone was falling head over heels in love with her!Enter Kansas City Chiefs legendary tight end Travis Kelce.The NFL star attended Swift’s concert at Arrowhead Stadium in July 2023.He prepared a custom friendship bracelet with his phone number.“I was disappointed that she doesn’t talk before or after her shows because she has to save her voice for the 44 songs she sings,” Kelce said on New Heights podcast in July 2023.“If you’re up on Taylor Swift concerts, there are friendship bracelets and I had received a bunch of them, but I wanted to give Taylor Swift with my number on it.”His bold public declaration of interest probably prompted her to reach out.They began talking privately and confirmed their fairytale romance in Sept. 2023. And the rest is history!Matty Healy emerges as the ultimate inspiration behind Taylor Swift’s TTPD.BETCHES’ Ilana Frost speculates“But Daddy I Love Him,” “Down Bad,” “Guilty As Sin?” “The Tortured Poets Department,” “Fresh Out The Slammer,”“I Can Fix Him [No Really I Can],” “loml,” “Fortnight,” and “The Smallest Man Who Ever Lived”are about Matty Healy.An insider told US Weekly that Matty Healy was worried about his story with Taylor Swift being shed in a negative light.But most TTPD songs are positive about him. Their relationship was fast but extremely passionate and real.As for The Life of a Showgirl? Which screams about the Kansas City Chiefs star player at the loudest possible decibels.PEOPLE’s Jordana Comiter speculates“The Fate of Ophelia,” “Opalite,” “Eldest Daughter,” “Wi$h Li$t,” “Wood,” and “Honey”are about Travis Kelce.“I have favorite words and favorite phrases and things that I will put in an endless file of lyrics that I’m just constantly going to and cherry picking from when I’m writing,”Taylor Swift said in a radio interview in Oct. 2025.“And I had written down the world ‘Opalite’ because I learned that it is actually a manmade opal. Opal can be manmade just like diamonds.Travis’ birthstone is an opal, so I have always fixated on that, I’ve always loved that stone.I thought it was kind of cool metaphor. [Opalite is] a manmade opal and happiness can also be manmade, too.So that’s kind of what the song is about, kind of the juxtaposition of those two.”She indirectly calls him her happiness. He is her happily ever after! And we cannot wait for them to already get married!
Article Summary: Eras Tour saved Taylor Swift from Joe Alwyn and Matty Healy and led her to savior Travis Kelce. The NFL star attended Swift’s concert at Arrowhead Stadium in July 2023. He prepared a custom friendship bracelet with his phone number. And the rest is history!

Article Title: CBS Sports analyst Russell Wilson manifested five dream girl non-negotiables and found Ciara three days later
Article Text:
CBS Sports analyst Russell Wilson manifested five dream girl non-negotiables and found Ciara three days later (Image via Getty: Russell Wilson and Ciara)

NFL legend Russell Wilson reveals it was love at first sight with singer-songwriter Ciara

Future Zahir Wilburn Wilson and unique worlds played a role

Woman of faith. Faithful. Independent. Similar to mom. Tilt the room when she walks in.Those were the five dream girl non-negotiables Russell Wilson jotted down as advised by his pastor.Three days later, he met Ciara . The girl of his dreams!When Russell Wilson met Ciara for the first time, the NFL legend was 26, and the singer-songwriter was 29.The retired NFL player knew exactly that she was the one the moment he met her. It was love at first sight for him.But the backstory that led him to her includes these five non-negotiables:Woman of faith. Faithful. Independent. Similar to mom. Tilt the room when she walks in.Over a decade ago, one Sunday, when Wilson was having dinner with his pastor, the pastor mentioned the idea of “non-negotiables.”Like the strict must-have qualities for a partner.Wilson treated marriage seriously like a life partnership or a business deal then.He decided to write down his own list of five things that he needed in his woman [dream girl / woman of his dreams].1. Woman of faith – a woman who believes in God.2. Faithful – someone who is loyal and honest and can be trustworthy3. Independent – someone who has her own strong identity, not just Russell Wilson’s wife.4. Similar to his mother, a nurturing, strong woman like his own mother, who cared for his dying father.5. Tilt the room when she walks in – someone with a powerful presence that changes the feel of a room the moment she walks in.He chose “five” non-negotiables because in Christianity, five means “grace.” He believes life and marriage require a lot of grace.He wrote the list, then prayed over it, and just three days later, he met Ciara for the very first time. The rest is history!When Russell Wilson met Ciara for the first time, Future was about nine months old.He told her, “No matter if you and I ever end up together, I’m gonna make sure I look out for the little man and help in whatever way you need.”They ended up spending over 11 years together. When he’s low, she lifts him up. When she’s low, he lifts her up.The CBS Sports analyst is amazed at the way their worlds collided.“Two young Black boy and girl from Richmond, Virginia, and from Atlanta, Georgia.When you think about how our worlds collided in such a way, there’s amazing responsibility and joy to that.That’s the part that you’re always learning, growing, and fighting for and doing. That’s the piece that makes it unique in itself,” Wilson said.
Article Summary: NFL legend Russell Wilson reveals it was love at first sight with singer- songwriter Ciara. The retired NFL player knew exactly that she was the one the moment he met her. He chose “five” non-negotiables because in Christianity, five means “grace”

Article Title: “People end up dying”: Terry Bradshaw drops a major hint about his broadcasting future amid FOX NFL Sunday uncertainty
Article Text:
Terry Bradshaw drops a major hint about his broadcasting future amid FOX NFL Sunday uncertainty(Getty)

Terry Bradshaw signals he is not ready to walk away from broadcasting amid FOX uncertainty

Terry Bradshaw has spent more than three decades as one of the faces of FOX NFL Sunday. Now, at 77, the Hall of Fame quarterback has offered his clearest update yet on what comes next.During an appearance on Sports Business Radio with host Brian Berger, Terry Bradshaw made it clear that retirement is not part of his plans. However, he also acknowledged that his future with FOX may not be entirely in his hands.Questions about Terry Bradshaw's future have followed him for years, but his latest comments gave fans a better picture of where he stands. During the Sports Business Radio interview, Bradshaw admitted that FOX could ultimately decide when his run on FOX NFL Sunday ends.“I may not be with FOX. That would be their call, not mine. But I'll still be speaking. Billy Graham said that the day that you retire is the day you start dying. And I do believe a lot of people, when they stop using their brain and keep their thought processes moving and advancing, for whatever reason, I do believe you age, and people end up dying. I mean, people die within a year after retirement. So, I don't wanna do that. I see myself staying fully active right up to the end.” Bradshaw told Brian Berger on Sports Business Radio.He continued: “I can't do that. My wife and I, we've been home now going on two weeks and we're going crazy. We're not used to being still. We were driving around yesterday, coming in from town. And I said, ‘Gee, we've got two weeks at home!’ We are never still.”That statement quickly grabbed attention because Bradshaw did not sound like someone preparing for retirement. In fact, he explained why stepping away from work does not appeal to him.During the same interview, Bradshaw referenced late evangelist Billy Graham while discussing retirement. He said many people lose purpose after they stop working and added that he wants to remain mentally active for as long as possible.The comments fit a pattern. Back in 2023, Bradshaw joked about dying on the set of FOX NFL Sunday. Then in 2025, fellow host Curt Menefee joked that Bradshaw would leave the show only in a hearse. While those remarks came with laughter, they reflected Bradshaw's long-standing belief that he wants to keep working.Still, the conversation around his future has grown louder. Several on-air mistakes over the past two seasons have sparked debate among viewers. During FOX's conference championship coverage earlier this year, Bradshaw appeared to mix up game discussions. He also incorrectly suggested that Green Bay Packers quarterback Jordan Love would welcome the absence of Micah Parsons, even though Parsons plays for Green Bay.Those moments fueled questions about whether FOX could eventually move in a different direction. Yet Bradshaw's latest remarks suggest he has no interest in slowing down.He reinforced that point when discussing life away from television. Bradshaw said he and his wife recently spent two weeks at home and quickly grew restless. For someone who helped build the modern NFL pregame show and has covered ten Super Bowls, staying busy remains part of the plan.Whether FOX keeps him on air or not, Bradshaw made one thing clear. He still sees a future in front of a microphone.
Article Summary: Terry Bradshaw has spent more than three decades as one of the faces of FOX NFL Sunday. At 77, the Hall of Fame quarterback has offered his clearest update yet on what comes next. Bradshaw said he and his wife recently spent two weeks at home and quickly grew restless.
